title: Senco Gold shares in focus as Q4 net profit soars 151% to Rs 157 crore, tops FY26 guidance
summary: Senco Gold reported a sharp rise in the March-quarter profit and revenue, aided by higher precious metal prices, strong wedding demand and robust retail sales growth. The company also expanded its showroom network and witnessed healthy traction in silver and diamond segments despite elevated gold prices impacting overall gold volumes.

title: Vegorama Punjabi shares to debut today. Check GMP ahead of debut
summary: Vegorama Punjabi Angithi's shares are poised for their stock market debut on Wednesday, with strong grey market indicators suggesting a healthy listing. The IPO, priced at Rs 77, saw a 12% premium in unofficial trading, hinting at a potential Rs 86 listing price. The company aims to utilize the Rs 38.38 crore raised for expansion.

title: JM Financial ARC top bidder for Rs 971-cr DLI debt
summary: JM Financial ARC has emerged as the preferred bidder for Distribution Logistics Infrastructure's ₹971 crore debt, offering ₹621 crore. Lenders, led by Bank of Baroda, will see a 64% recovery. This follows a failed settlement attempt with promoters, with the process expected to take more time.

title: NSE bars Yes Securities from adding new clients
summary: Yes Securities faces a three-month ban on onboarding new clients and a ₹1 lakh penalty from the NSE. The brokerage was penalized for passing on upfront or peak margin penalties to its clients. The order was issued by the National Stock Exchange of India on May 26.

title: Stocks in news: Coal India, Marico, Fino Payments Bank, L&T, Physicswallah
summary: Indian markets closed lower amid global volatility and geopolitical concerns, with analysts expecting continued choppiness due to monthly expiry. Several stocks like Coal India, Marico, and L&T are in focus due to news developments, including government stake sales, new product launches, and order wins.

title: Negative cues from GIFT Nifty signal muted opening for D-Street
summary: The Nifty slipped below 24,000, closing at 23,913 amid monthly F&O expiry and profit booking. Analysts suggest a gradual up-move could continue if crude oil prices remain soft, but geopolitical risks from US-Iran tensions are likely to keep investors cautious.

title: Listed brokers, exchanges rake in profits even as war clouds linger
summary: India's listed brokers and exchanges reported robust March quarter earnings, driven by a surge in Margin Trading Facility (MTF) and a favorable base effect. Increased market volatility, strong commodity trading, and elevated VIX levels also boosted volumes. Brokers are diversifying income streams beyond core broking, evolving into 'pseudo-NBFCs' with offerings like wealth management and insurance.

title: AI Inside: Taiwan goes past India to number 5 in global m-cap league
summary: Taiwan has surpassed India to become the fifth largest stock market globally. This shift is driven by a strong AI-fueled rally. Taiwan's market capitalization now stands higher than India's. This surge is largely attributed to the performance of key semiconductor companies. Foreign investors have been reducing their exposure to India. The global appetite for AI-related firms is reshaping market rankings.

title: Sebi turns to ETFs, derivatives for debt market push
summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) is set to introduce bond ETFs and derivatives to boost the corporate debt market. The regulator is also reviewing listing obligations for debt-only entities and exploring a pilot for tokenizing corporate bonds to enhance liquidity and investor access.

title: Stock Radar: Narayana Hrudayalaya stock breaks out from inverse head & shoulder pattern; time to buy?
summary: Narayana Hrudayalaya stock shows a strong bullish breakout. Experts suggest buying for a target above 2,100 levels in the next 1-2 months. The stock has formed higher lows and moved above key moving averages. Momentum indicators are supportive, indicating strengthening trend. Immediate support is expected near 1820-1840 levels. Accumulation is recommended in the 1900-1920 range.

title: ICICI Bank adds most credit cards in April amid rising competition
summary: Axis Bank added 63,446 cards in April, less than half its February tally. Kotak Mahindra Bank remained steady at 33,038 additions. "We are focused on growing the business and growing it with the right set of customers in a profitable way," said ICICI Bank Group chief financial officer Anindya Banerjee. "We have been seeing reasonably steady new customer acquisition and would hope to see better numbers in terms of growth."

title: Irdai links executive pay at insurers to elimination of unfair practices
summary: The move is part of new KMP performance evaluation rules, under which the regulator has made 50% of key performance indicators (KPIs) mandatory and far more prescriptive than earlier, according to industry executives. For the remaining 50% weightage in performance assessment, the nomination and remuneration committee or board may assign additional role-specific parameters aligned with the insurer's business plan, with the metrics and evaluation criteria required to be clearly defined upfront in the remuneration policy.

title: Gold Holds Loss as Traders Weigh Prospects for US-Iran Deal
summary: Gold held a loss, as fresh clashes in the Persian Gulf weighed on hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: Emerging Currencies Slide as Iran War ‘Confusion’ Jolts Markets
summary: Most emerging-market currencies weakened on Tuesday as overnight clashes near the Strait of Hormuz offset signs of progress toward a US-Iran peace deal, even as developing-nation stocks rose on optimism around artificial intelligence.

title: Startups chase GIFT City licences to win NRI investors, but will the rush last?
summary: Platforms are pivoting to a single regulated stack to simplify cross-border flows as market headwinds drive a defensive shift in overseas Indians' wealth. But some caution this increased appetite may be cyclical rather than permanent.

title: Himalaya Wealth launches  ₹500 crore SME fund to back manufacturing, deeptech firms
summary: The Category I AIF will target profitable growth-stage SMEs in sectors including aerospace, defence, industrial automation and healthcare, amid rising investor interest in manufacturing-led businesses.

title: Focusing on profitable growth: LTM CEO Venu Lambu
summary: LTIMindtree CEO Venu Lambu is betting on profitable growth despite AI concerns and weak investor sentiment. The company posted its fastest growth in three years and is expanding globally through its first acquisition, even as shares remain under pressure.

title: Young buyers lift India to #3 spot for Swiss watchmaker Tissot
summary: Tissot chief executive Sylvain Dolla says India’s young luxury consumers are driving strong growth, pushing the country ahead of France and Switzerland for the watch brand. Tissot sales in India have risen over 35% annually in the last three years.

title: Others open their wallets for buybacks. Cognizant turned to credit.
summary: In Nasdaq filings last week, the US-headquartered IT services giant said it will draw $1 billion for the buyback from a $1.85 billion revolving credit facility. Cognizant had secured the credit line in 2014 to support the $2.7 billion acquisition of Trizetto, a healthcare-focused IT firm.

title: EPFO 3.0: No paperwork, no waiting — instant EPF withdrawal via ATM and UPI; what actually changes for EPF users
summary: EPFO subscribers may soon withdraw funds directly to their bank accounts via UPI, eliminating employer sign-off and processing delays. Labor Minister Mansukh Mandaviya announced the initiative, which allows instant QR code transactions at ATMs, benefiting over seven crore members.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Railway union proposes 5-fitment-factor formula; salaries may jump over 400% — check calculator
summary: The IRTSA has proposed distinct fitment factors for various employee levels under the 8th Pay Commission, suggesting a range from 2.92 to 4.38. This could lead to significant salary increases, with some employees seeing hikes exceeding 400%.

title: Family feud over inheritance? Here’s how to avoid years of litigation
summary: When a business patriarch dies without a Will or succession plan, disputes can quickly spiral. Experts explain how families can avoid years of costly litigation.

title: Bank holiday today, May 27: Are SBI, HDFC Bank, and other banks closed for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha? – Check list
summary: Banks may be closed on May 27 or 28 for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha, depending on the state. Public holidays apply to central and most state government offices. Online banking and ATMs remain operational, allowing customers to perform transactions despite bank closures.

title: China industrial profits jump 24.7% in April, fastest gain in over two years despite headwinds
summary: China’s industrial profits grew at their fastest pace in over two years in April, buoyed by stronger exports, higher producer prices and gains in upstream industries.

title: European companies double down on China manufacturing despite EU de-risking push
summary: Low manufacturing costs in China are keeping many European businesses' supply chains in the country despite pressure in the EU to reduce overseas reliance.
